target.title: fix(process): guard stdin writable to avoid gateway crash on not-found binaries
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: `createChildAdapter` throws `EPIPE` when spawning a binary that doesn't exist (e.g. `gemini` not found), because the spawned process has a `stdin` stream that is not writable.
- Why it matters: On hosts where google-cli is installed in a non-standard path (post-onboarding), the agent crashes with an uncaught exception instead of falling back gracefully.
- What changed: Added `child.stdin.writable` guard in `createChildAdapter` before attempting to write input to stdin.
- What did NOT change: No changes to process spawning logic, fallback mechanism, or stream handling for normal (found) binaries.

## Change Type (select all touched areas)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: When a binary is not found (e.g. `spawn: command not found`), the child process is still created with a `stdin` handle, but the handle is not writable. The existing code only checked `if (child.stdin)` before writing, missing the non-writable case.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No `writable` property check on `child.stdin` before write/end.
- Contributing context: google-cli onboarding installs the binary in a non-standard path; the CLI path resolution doesn't find it, but the spawned process still has a stdin stub.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.test.ts` — new test "does not throw when stdin exists but is not writable"
- Scenario the test should lock in: spawning a non-existent binary results in a child with `stdin` that is not writable; `createChildAdapter` should not throw.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Unit test mocks `spawnWithFallback` and directly asserts the adapter doesn't throw; no E2E needed for this edge case.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a previously untested edge case.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — new test added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).
If none, write `None`.

- Before: agent crashes with uncaught `EPIPE` exception when CLI binary is missing/unavailable.
- After: agent handles the missing-stdin case gracefully; no crash.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
Before:
[user action: onboarding google-cli on non-standard path] -> [spawn "gemini" fails, child.stdin exists but !writable] -> [child.stdin.write() throws EPIPE] -> [uncaught exception, agent crash]

After:
[user action: onboarding google-cli on non-standard path] -> [spawn "gemini" fails, child.stdin exists but !writable] -> [guard: child.stdin.writable === false] -> [skip stdin write, no throw] -> [graceful handling / fallback]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)  
- Runtime/container: Node.js (Docker: `node` user, `/app/dist/supervisor-*.js`)
- Model/provider: `google-gemini-cli` / `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`
- Integration/channel: Slack (Socket Mode)
- Relevant config (redacted): google-cli installed in non-standard PATH, `gemini` binary not on PATH

### Steps

1. Onboard google-cli to a non-standard installation path (not on system PATH).
2. Start an agent session with `provider=google-gemini-cli model=gemini-3.1-pro-preview`.
3. Send a message that triggers a CLI exec.

### Expected

- Agent handles the missing binary gracefully (fallback or clear error).

### Actual

- Uncaught `EPIPE` exception: `gemini: 1: exec: gemini: not found` followed by `Error: write EPIPE`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording   
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `child.stdin` exists but `writable === false` — no throw.
  - `child.stdin` exists and `writable === true` — normal write path (existing behavior preserved).
  - `child.stdin` is `null` — no change (existing branch handles it).
- Edge cases checked: `stdin` exists as non-boolean truthy value with `writable: false` via `Object.defineProperty`.
- What you did **not** verify: Live E2E with actual google-cli on non-standard path; Docker container execution.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration 

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`) 
- Migration needed? (`No`)   
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Skipping stdin write silently for processes that _should_ accept input but have a non-writable stdin.
  - Mitigation: The only scenario where `stdin` exists but `!writable` is a failed spawn (binary not found). The fallback mechanism already handles this case — the spawned process exits immediately with an error code, and the supervisor reports the failure. No legitimate input-bearing process is affected.

target.title: Sessions page: use numeric input for Limit field
target.body: Summary Fixes the Sessions page Limit field so it uses a numeric input instead of accepting arbitrary text. Problem The field was implemented as a plain text input and passed raw string values into filter state. This allowed invalid values such as abc or eyertyl to be entered without validation. Changes changed Limit input to type="number" added numeric constraints with min="1" and step="1" parsed input before passing it into onFiltersChange fall back to undefined for invalid or empty values Steps to Reproduce Open Control -> Sessions Enter a non-numeric value into the Limit field Observe that the old implementation accepts invalid text without validation Expected Behavior Limit should accept only valid numeric input. Actual Behavior The field accepts arbitrary text and forwards it as filter state. Result The field now behaves like a numeric limit input and prevents inval id text-based values from being used.

target.title: fix(gateway): aggregate usage cost across agents
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets: - Problem: `usage.cost` scanned only one default transcript directory when callers did not pass `agentId`, while `sessions.usage` already discovered sessions across configured agents. - Why it matters: Control UI and menu usage views can show empty or partial daily cost data in multi-agent deployments even when session usage exists under other agents. - What changed: resolve the `usage.cost` agent scope from configured gateway agents, merge daily/totals across agents, include the resolved scope in the cache key, and preserve date-only daily labels in the Control UI. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not implement the dedicated `usage.jsonl` storage proposal from #13219 or a separate cost-unit normalization pass; it addresses the confirmed cross-agent aggregation/scope mismatch reported in #41986. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #13219 - Addresses the confirmed aggregation-scope bug from #41986 - Supersedes #42063 by @stablegenius49; this PR carries forward its core `usage.cost` aggregation direction with additional cache-scope, explicit-agent, UI date-label, and partial-failure coverage. - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: `usage.cost` called `loadCostUsageSummary` without an agent scope, which resolves through the single-agent/default transcript directory instead of the configured gateway agent set. - Missing detection / guardrail: gateway tests covered multi-agent discovery for `sessions.usage`, but not the sibling `usage.cost` aggregate path, non-`main` default agent ids, or browser-visible daily usage rendering for date-only buckets. - Contributing context (if known): #13219 describes a broader usage tracking enhancement; this is a narrower current-state aggregate fix. #42063 previously proposed the core aggregation direction, and this PR supersedes it with broader scope validation, cache scoping, UI, and partial-failure coverage. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/views/usage-metrics.node.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: `usage.cost` without `agentId` calls the cost summary loader once per configured agent and merges daily/totals; explicit `agentId` remains scoped; configured default agents do not need to be named `main`; date-only daily buckets render with the same calendar day in the Control UI. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the gateway handler boundary, mocked transcript-cost seam, and UI date formatting behavior without needing real transcript files. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for multi-agent `usage.cost`. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Multi-agent deployments should see `usage.cost` totals and daily entries aggregated across configured agents when no `agentId` is requested. Explicit `agentId` requests remain scoped to that agent. The Control UI daily usage chart should no longer show `No data` when cross-agent session usage exists, and date-only daily buckets no longer shift one day backward for users west of UTC. ## Diagram (if applicable) For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`. ```text Before: Control UI -> usage.cost(no agentId) -> one default transcript directory only -> Daily Usage: No data After: Control UI -> usage.cost(no agentId) -> configured agents -> merged daily/totals -> Daily Token Usage ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? Yes - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `usage.cost` now reads the same configured agent session scopes that `sessions.usage` already reads. Explicit `agentId` requests are validated and remain scoped, and cache keys include the resolved agent scope. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: host Node `v25.9.0`, pnpm `10.33.0` - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Gateway usage RPC and Control UI - Relevant config (redacted): `agents.list = [{ id: "ops", default: true }, { id: "opus" }]` ### Steps 1. Configure multiple agents with usage-bearing transcripts outside a single default transcript directory. 2. Request `usage.cost` without `agentId`. 3. Compare returned daily/totals with per-agent cost summaries and the Control UI Usage page. ### Expected - The response includes merged daily/totals across configured agents. - The Control UI daily usage chart renders the merged daily bucket instead of `No data`. ### Actual - Before this change, only one default transcript directory was scanned, so the Control UI could show `Daily Usage` / `No data` while `sessions.usage` showed activity. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Trace/log summary: - Before/live gateway `18789`: `usage.cost` for `2026-04-22` returned `daily: []` and zero totals, while `sessions.usage` for the same range returned a populated daily aggregate. - After/PR gateway `18790`: `usage.cost` for `2026-04-22` returned a populated daily bucket with about `117M` tokens and by-type breakdown. - Chrome MCP visual smoke on the PR gateway showed `Daily Token Usage`, `Apr 22`, `April 22, 2026`, and token-type breakdown rows instead of `No data`. - Chrome MCP visual smoke also verified the `Cost` toggle before/after: the official gateway still showed the daily chart as `No data`, while the PR gateway rendered `Daily Cost`, `Apr 22`, `$111.24`, and `COST BY TYPE`. - Screenshots are embedded in this PR description and are not committed to the codebase. Screenshots: Before, current official build showing the scrolled `Daily Usage` section with `No data`: After, PR gateway `18790` showing the scrolled `Daily Token Usage` section populated for `Apr 22`: Before, current official build with `Cost` selected still showing the daily chart as `No data`: After, PR gateway `18790` with `Cost` selected showing `Daily Cost` populated for `Apr 22`: ## Prior Work / Credit This PR supersedes #42063 by @stablegenius49 and carries forward its core direction: make `usage.cost` aggregate across configured gateway agents. The implementation here expands that work with explicit `agentId` validation/scoping, agent-scoped cache keys, partial per-agent failure handling, non-`main` agent coverage, UI date-label handling, and visual verification. Suggested maintainer landing credit, if squashing or direct-landing this PR: `(thanks @stablegenius49)` or `Co-authored-by: stablegenius49 <185121704+stablegenius49@users.noreply.github.com>`. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: multi-agent `usage.cost` aggregate, explicit `agentId` scoping, invalid/out-of-scope explicit `agentId` rejection, cache behavior with agent-scoped cache keys, configured default agent not named `main`, first configured agent fallback, no-agent fallback to `main`, discovered disk agents when no list constrains the gateway, Control UI daily token rendering, and Control UI daily cost rendering against a PR-branch gateway. - Edge cases checked: bounded cache eviction, in-flight cache coalescing, gateway typecheck/lint/import-cycle guards, date-only bucket rendering under `America/Sao_Paulo` timezone. - What you did **not** verify: no known unverified items for this PR scope. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: more transcript directories may be scanned for unscoped `usage.cost` requests. - Mitigation: the gateway already uses the same configured agent list for `sessions.usage`, and `usage.cost` remains cached with the resolved agent scope included in the cache key. ## AI Assistance - AI-assisted: Yes, implemented with OpenAI Codex. - Degree of testing: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, targeted gateway usage/UI Vitest shards (`pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.cost-usage-cache.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage-metrics.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/usage-helpers.node.test.ts test/official-channel-catalog.test.ts`), `pnpm check:changed`, `pnpm build`, Chrome MCP visual smoke against a PR-branch gateway including `Tokens` and `Cost` modes, and `codex review --base origin/main` at refreshed head `c613510233` with no actionable findings. Current mergeable head `e14c554f32` was refreshed with a normal merge from `origin/main` and revalidated with `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, the focused usage/UI tests, `pnpm check:changed`, and `pnpm build`. - Session logs/prompts: not attached. - Understanding: I reviewed the agent-scope and gateway usage paths; the production change aggregates the existing transcript-backed cost summaries across resolved gateway agent ids, preserves explicit `agentId` scoping, and fixes date-only daily label rendering. - Local Codex review: `codex review --base origin/main` was run locally and reported no actionable findings.

target.title: Feishu: harden comment reply delivery and bot identity refresh
target.body:
## Summary

  - Problem: Feishu comment handling could process notices not meant for the current bot, duplicate replies after tool-visible replies, fail on raw `<` / `>` text, and reuse stale bot identity.
  - Why it matters: This caused misrouted comment handling, duplicate user-visible replies, comment write failures, and unstable multi-account behavior.
  - What changed: Added recipient gating for comment notices, suppressed automatic final replies after successful tool delivery in the same comment flow, escaped angle brackets in comment writes, isolated typing-reaction state by `accountId`, and forced fresh bot identity probes on startup/reload with a credential-aware cache key.

  ## Change Type (select all)

  - [x] Bug fix
  - [ ] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
  - [ ] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra

  ## Linked Issue/PR

  - Related #
  - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  ## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: Comment handling lacked strict recipient validation, stale identity invalidation, and cross-path guards between tool-visible replies and automatic final replies.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: No regression coverage for recipient gating, duplicate-reply suppression, escaping, multi-account typing isolation, or fresh probe behavior.
  - Contributing context (if known): Comment threads mix automatic final replies, tool-visible replies, and multi-account runtime state.

  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [x] Unit test
    - [x] Seam / integration test
    - [ ] End-to-end test
    - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file:
    - `extensions/feishu/src/comment-dispatcher.test.ts`
    - `extensions/feishu/src/comment-reaction.test.ts`
    - `extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts`
    - `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.comment.test.ts`
    - `extensions/feishu/src/probe.test.ts`
    - `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.startup.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: Recipient-gated comment notices, tool-reply suppression of duplicate automatic finals, angle-bracket escaping, multi-account typing isolation, and fresh bot identity probes.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  - Feishu comment notices are handled only when addressed to the current bot.
  - Tool-visible replies in the same comment flow now suppress later duplicate automatic final replies.
  - Raw `<` / `>` in comment text no longer fail Feishu comment writes.
  - Multi-account comment typing reactions no longer interfere with each other.
  - Startup/reload now refreshes bot identity more reliably.

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```text
  Before:
  [comment notice] -> [broad/stale bot match] -> [tool reply] -> [automatic final] -> [duplicate/misdirected reply]

  After:
  [comment notice] -> [current bot match] -> [tool reply or automatic final] -> [delivery guard] -> [single intended reply]
`````
  ## Security Impact (required)

  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) Yes
  - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
  - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation:
      - Bot probe cache keys now include a credential fingerprint in memory only.
      - Startup/reload can force a fresh call to the existing Feishu bot ping endpoint.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: local dev shell
  - Integration/channel (if any): Feishu Drive comments

  ### Steps

  1. Trigger Feishu Drive comment notices, including one not addressed to the current bot.
  2. Trigger tool-visible replies in the same comment thread.
  3. Test angle-bracket comment text, multi-account typing cleanup, and startup/reload identity refresh.

  ### Expected

  - Only intended comment notices are handled.
  - No duplicate automatic final reply after a tool-visible reply.
  - Comment writes with < / > succeed.
  - Multi-account typing state stays isolated.

  - Before this change, misrouted events, duplicate replies, write failures, and stale/mixed state could happen.

  - Result: Test Files 6 passed (6), Tests 67 passed (67)

  ## Human Verification (required)

  - Verified scenarios: Manually verified Feishu comment recipient gating, duplicate-reply suppression after tool-visible replies, angle-bracket comment writes, multi-account typing-reaction isolation, and bot identity refresh behavior; also reran targeted Feishu regression tests locally.
  - Edge cases checked: Wrong-recipient notices, missing `to_user_id.open_id`, whole-comment fallback, reply-forbidden fallback, missing `accountId` ambient cleanup, and changed credentials with fresh probe.

This is a compatibility behavior for existing comment-thread follow-up flows and does not  require caller migration.

target.title: fix(msteams): paginate thread replies and keep recent context
target.body:
Title: fix(msteams): paginate thread replies and keep recent context

## Summary

- Problem: Teams thread context enrichment only used the first Graph replies page, so long threads could skew toward the oldest replies.
- Why it matters: the bot could miss the newest thread context, which is usually the most relevant context for a channel-thread reply.
- What changed: `fetchThreadReplies` now follows `@odata.nextLink` under a hard page cap and returns the most recent bounded reply window instead of only the first page.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no live-tenant Graph validation, no changes to higher-level message-handler behavior, and no prompt-formatting changes beyond the selected reply window.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the Teams thread helper fetched only the initial Graph replies page even when Graph exposed additional pages via `@odata.nextLink`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no unit coverage for paginated reply retrieval or for preferring the newest bounded reply window.
- Contributing context (if known): the Graph replies endpoint is oldest-first and does not support `$orderby`, so a single-page implementation silently favors stale context on long threads.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/msteams/src/graph-thread.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when Graph returns multiple reply pages for a channel thread, the helper follows pagination and keeps the most recent bounded reply window rather than only the first page.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is isolated to the Graph reply helper and can be reproduced deterministically with mocked paginated Graph responses.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this change.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Long Teams channel threads can now contribute newer reply context to the bot instead of only the oldest reply page.
- Behavior stays best-effort: if Graph pagination or fetches fail, the surrounding handler still degrades gracefully as before.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[long Teams thread] -> [fetch first replies page only] -> [oldest replies injected] -> [bot may miss latest context]

After:
[long Teams thread] -> [follow paginated replies with hard cap] -> [keep most recent bounded window] -> [bot sees newer thread context]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (Yes)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Risk: this can issue additional Graph requests for long threads.
  - Mitigation: pagination is capped at 10 pages and the helper still returns only the requested bounded reply window.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: repo-local Node via `pnpm openclaw`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): MSTeams Graph helper unit tests
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Mock the first Graph replies page with `reply-1`, `reply-2`, and an `@odata.nextLink`.
2. Mock the second page with `reply-3`, `reply-4`.
3. Call `fetchThreadReplies(..., limit=2)`.

### Expected

- The helper should follow the next link and return the newest bounded window: `reply-3`, `reply-4`.

### Actual

- Before the fix, the helper would only use the first page and return `reply-1`, `reply-2`, dropping the newer page entirely.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: targeted unit run for `extensions/msteams/src/graph-thread.test.ts`; paginated reply path; hard page-cap path; existing thread-helper tests remained green.
- Edge cases checked: missing `value`, min/max limit clamping, bounded pagination cap.
- What you did **not** verify: live Microsoft Graph behavior against a real Teams tenant or real long thread pagination ordering/latency.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Microsoft Graph pagination behavior for long Teams threads has not been validated against a real tenant in this change.
  - Mitigation: the PR makes the assumption explicit, keeps the change local to the helper, and adds deterministic regression tests around the intended pagination behavior.
- Risk: extra Graph fetches could increase latency on large threads.
  - Mitigation: page following is hard-capped and still returns only a bounded recent window.

target.title: feat(messages): add responseFooter and enrich /usage full
target.body:
## Summary

_AI-assisted PR. Prepared with AI assistance against a repo snapshot; targeted validation only. See Repro + Verification and Human Verification for exact coverage._

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: OpenClaw had a native `messages.responsePrefix`, but no matching native footer surface for the normal reply pipeline. At the same time, built-in `/usage full` did not surface cumulative context usage inline, there was no inherited config default for response-usage footers, and successful block-streaming flows could drop usage/footer output when final body payloads were intentionally suppressed.
- Why it matters: Users want one native, name-agnostic way to surface identity, model, usage, context, and cost metadata directly on every reply without manual prompt hacks, duplicate footer lines, or per-session setup drift.
- What changed: This PR adds `messages.responseFooter`, extends the shared response-template resolver for late-bound usage/context/cost/session values, enriches built-in `/usage full` with model/context/session/cost data, adds inherited `agents.defaults.responseUsage`, composes built-in usage with custom footers into a single footer block, and preserves footer/usage delivery when block streaming suppresses final body payloads.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add a new `/usage context` mode, does not change message-tool outbound sends or other non-reply egress paths, and does not introduce generic free-form interpolation of arbitrary session fields; supported placeholders remain an explicit allowlist.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #40215
- Closes #12578
- Related #24014
- Related #17832
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

Note: `#24014` and `#17832` are currently closed upstream, so they are referenced as related context even though this PR implements the requested behavior in those threads. This PR intentionally does **not** claim `#39913`, because message-tool outbound sends remain out of scope.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: Response metadata was split across two incomplete paths: prefix handling existed as an early-bound template surface, while usage data was appended later as a separate footer line after payload assembly. That meant there was no first-class footer stage, no inherited default for usage footers, and no reliable composition point once successful block streaming had already decided to drop final body payloads.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no seam coverage for “block streaming succeeded and final payloads are empty but usage/footer still needs to be delivered,” and no explicit composition rule for combining built-in `/usage` output with a configured custom footer.
- Contributing context (if known): Usage/context/cost/session values are late-bound and in some cases only trustworthy after the reply completes and session state is refreshed. Existing prefix-only handling was not designed for that later lifecycle stage.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.footer.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-reply-pipeline.test.ts`
  - `src/config/runtime-overrides.test.ts`
  - `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts`
  - `src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `/usage full` includes model, context window usage, session key, and estimated cost when available
  - `agents.defaults.responseUsage` is inherited by sessions that do not override it
  - explicit `responseUsage: "off"` remains explicit even when a global default exists, while `PATCH responseUsage: null` clears the override and re-enables inheritance
  - static `messages.responseFooter` values fold with built-in usage into one appended footer block
  - usage-aware footers suppress the separate built-in usage line to avoid duplicate footer lines
  - successful block streaming still returns a trailing footer/usage payload when final body payloads are intentionally suppressed
  - response prefixes do not leak raw late-bound `{cost}` / `{usageLine}` / context placeholders before those values are known
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The risk is in formatting, inheritance, and reply-payload composition. Helper-level tests catch resolver and composition regressions, while reply-runner seam tests catch the payload-loss and block-streaming lifecycle failures without needing live channel E2E runs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing prefix-template coverage already exercised the shared resolver pattern; this PR extends that surface and adds footer- and usage-specific cases.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- New optional `messages.responseFooter` config for the normal reply pipeline.
- `messages.responsePrefix` and `messages.responseFooter` now share the same case-insensitive, allowlisted template resolver for placeholders such as `{model}`, `{provider}`, `{identityName}`, `{effort}`, `{input}`, `{output}`, `{total}`, `{cacheRead}`, `{cacheWrite}`, `{context}`, `{contextMax}`, `{contextPercent}`, `{cost}`, `{usageLine}`, and `{session}`.
- Built-in `/usage full` is enriched to include cumulative context usage, model label, session key, and estimated cost when available.
- New inherited config: `agents.defaults.responseUsage` (`off` | `tokens` | `full`). Sessions without an explicit override inherit this default.
- Session-level explicit `responseUsage: "off"` is preserved even when a global default exists. `PATCH responseUsage: null` still clears the explicit override and returns the session to inherited behavior.
- When both `/usage` and `messages.responseFooter` are enabled:
  - static footers compose with built-in usage into one appended footer block
  - usage-aware custom footers suppress the separate built-in usage line
  - cost-only custom footers still compose with built-in usage unless `{usageLine}` is explicitly included
- In successful block-streaming flows, usage/footer text can still be emitted as a trailing payload even when the final body payload is intentionally suppressed.
- For streamed prefixes, unresolved late-bound usage/context/cost placeholders are blanked instead of leaking raw `{cost}`-style tokens.
- Added `docs/response-footer.md` documenting config, placeholders, composition rules, and examples.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
Before:
[user message]
  -> [reply generated]
  -> [optional responsePrefix applied]
  -> [optional /usage line appended late]
  -> [block streaming may suppress final payloads]
  -> [custom footer unavailable natively, or usage/footer can be lost or duplicated]

After:
[user message]
  -> [reply generated]
  -> [early-bound prefix resolved]
  -> [late-bound usage/context/cost/session template context resolved]
  -> [built-in usage line formatted]
  -> [responseFooter composed with or against usage]
  -> [footer block appended to last text payload or emitted as trailing payload]
  -> [reply sent without duplicate footer lines]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node/TypeScript repo snapshot in an offline CAAS container
- Model/provider: mocked reply-runner and gateway paths with synthetic provider/model/usage metadata
- Integration/channel (if any): normal reply pipeline, successful block-streaming reply pipeline, gateway session patch path, and TUI `/usage` toggle path; no live external channel in this container
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "responseUsage": "full"
    },
    "list": [{ "id": "main", "identity": { "name": "Jarvis" } }]
  },
  "messages": {
    "responsePrefix": "*{identityName}:* {cost}",
    "responseFooter": "{usageLine}\n— {identityName}"
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure `agents.defaults.responseUsage` and optionally configure `messages.responseFooter`.
2. Trigger a normal reply, a successful block-streaming reply, and one or more `/usage` mode changes (`off`, `tokens`, `full`).
3. Inspect the final reply payloads and the effective session `responseUsage` state.

### Expected

- Sessions without an explicit override inherit `agents.defaults.responseUsage`.
- `/usage full` includes per-message tokens plus cumulative context, model, session, and estimated cost when available.
- Static custom footers fold with built-in usage into one footer block, while usage-aware footers suppress the separate built-in usage line.
- Successful block streaming does not drop footer/usage output just because the final body payload was suppressed.
- Prefixes do not leak raw late-bound placeholders before values are known.

### Actual

- In the covered paths, inherited usage defaults, explicit `off`, and clear-to-inherit (`null`) semantics behave as expected.
- `/usage full` is enriched with context/model/session/cost data in the covered reply paths.
- Footer composition follows the intended static-vs-usage-aware rules, and successful block streaming still returns footer/usage content as a trailing payload.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Representative snippets from the targeted coverage for this branch:

```text
Enriched built-in /usage full:
  Usage: 12k in / 450 out · cache 2.0k cached / 300 new · ctx 45k/200k (23%) · model gpt-5.4 · session `agent:main:webchat:dm:123`
```

```text
Static footer composed with built-in usage:
  ok

  Usage: 12 in / 3 out
  — footer
```

```text
Usage-aware footer suppressing duplicate built-in usage:
  ok

  ↑12 ↓3
```

```text
Successful block streaming with final body suppressed:
  streamed chunk: Hello
  trailing payload returned: Usage: 12 in / 3 out · cache 4 cached / 2 new
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - reviewed the touched config/type/schema/help/label surfaces for `messages.responseFooter` and `agents.defaults.responseUsage`
  - reviewed the reply-runner composition path for normal replies and the success path where block streaming suppresses final body payloads
  - inspected the new resolver/composition tests for footer, usage, inheritance, and prefix late-binding behavior
  - ran `git diff --check`
  - parsed changed TypeScript files successfully with the TypeScript compiler API in the offline container
  - manually exercised targeted helper/runtime behavior for prefix soft-blanking, cost-only footer composition, and enriched full usage-line formatting
- Edge cases checked:
  - explicit `off` versus inherited default
  - `PATCH responseUsage: null` re-enables inheritance
  - static footer versus usage-aware footer versus cost-only footer composition
  - blank or whitespace-only footer templates emit nothing
  - unresolved footer placeholders remain literal, while unresolved late-bound prefix placeholders are blanked
  - successful block streaming can return only a trailing footer payload without prepending a verbose new-session notice
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`
  - live Telegram/Discord/WhatsApp channel behavior
  - message-tool outbound send behavior (`#39913` remains intentionally out of scope)
  - screenshot-based verification in a running OpenClaw instance

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No review conversations exist yet because this PR body is being prepared before the PR is opened. Once the PR is live, the author should update these checkboxes based on the actual review state.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk:
  - Enabling both built-in `/usage` and `messages.responseFooter` could produce confusing duplicate footer lines.
  - Mitigation: Added explicit composition rules and tests that suppress the separate built-in usage line when the footer already consumes usage-aware placeholders.
- Risk:
  - Late-bound usage/context/cost values could be missing or stale when templates render.
  - Mitigation: The footer path resolves against late-bound reply/session data, prefers refreshed persisted session state when available, and soft-blanks unresolved late-bound prefix placeholders instead of leaking raw tokens.
- Risk:
  - Block-streaming channels may render the usage/footer as a separate trailing message rather than mutating the already-streamed body.
  - Mitigation: The implementation preserves delivery without trying to retroactively rewrite the streamed content, and dedicated reply-runner coverage locks in the suppressed-final-payload success path.
- Risk:
  - Inherited default semantics could confuse users if explicit `off` is not distinguishable from “unset.”
  - Mitigation: Explicit `off` is preserved in session state and TUI patching, while `PATCH responseUsage: null` remains the clear-to-inherit path; both behaviors are covered by tests and docs.

target.title: fix(memory): block symlink escapes in memory_get
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `memory_get` could accept a path that lexically stayed under the workspace or an allowed extra path, but escaped through an intermediate directory symlink.
- Why it matters: an agent using `memory_get` could be tricked into pulling content from outside the workspace or QMD memory boundary, which means normal memory lookups could unexpectedly surface unrelated local files instead of only the notes the user intended memory tools to access.
- What changed: resolve target files and allowed roots to real paths before reading, then reject reads whose canonical path falls outside the allowed root; applied to both builtin memory reads and QMD-backed reads.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to memory search ranking, indexing behavior, prompt logic, or non-memory file access surfaces.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] API / contracts

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the read path checks only validated the lexical path prefix and direct symlink files, but did not verify that the resolved real path still stayed inside the allowed workspace / extraPath / QMD collection root.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test covered an intermediate directory symlink that escaped the allowed root.
- Contributing context (if known): builtin and QMD read paths each enforced path rules separately, so both needed the same canonical-path containment check.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.read-file.test.ts`
  - `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: reject `memory_get` reads when a path under `memory/`, an allowed extra path, or a QMD collection crosses the boundary through an intermediate directory symlink.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in path resolution and file-read authorization, so direct read-path tests cover it without requiring full indexing or end-to-end setup.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing tests already covered direct symlink files; this PR extends coverage to directory symlink escapes.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`memory_get` now rejects markdown paths that escape the allowed workspace / extra path / QMD collection through intermediate directory symlinks.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
memory_get("memory/linked/secret.md")
-> lexical prefix check passes
-> linked/ resolves outside workspace
-> file is read

After:
memory_get("memory/linked/secret.md")
-> lexical prefix check passes
-> realpath resolves outside allowed root
-> request fails with "path required"
```
## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `Yes`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This narrows the readable file scope for `memory_get` to the canonical allowed roots and blocks previously unintended cross-boundary reads through directory symlinks.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node/Bun repo test runner
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): memory core / QMD
- Relevant config (redacted): default local test config

### Steps

1. Create a markdown file outside the workspace or outside an allowed extra path / QMD collection root.
2. Add an intermediate directory symlink inside `memory/`, an allowed extra path, or a QMD collection that points to that outside directory.
3. Run `memory_get` on the symlinked markdown path.

### Expected

- The read is rejected with `path required`.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the read could succeed because only the lexical path prefix was checked.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - builtin memory reads reject directory symlink escapes
  - extra-path memory reads reject nested directory symlink escapes
  - QMD reads reject directory symlink escapes
- Edge cases checked:
  - direct symlink-file rejection still holds
  - transient file disappearance path still returns empty text correctly after switching reads to canonical paths
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full `pnpm test`
  - full `pnpm check`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: setups that used a directory symlink inside `memory/`, an allowed extra path, or a QMD collection as an implicit way to reach files outside the configured memory roots will now fail on `memory_get`.
  - Mitigation: supported outside-workspace reads should be configured explicitly through `memorySearch.extraPaths` or QMD collections; this change aligns runtime reads with the documented memory-root contract and adds regression coverage for the escaped-symlink case.

target.title: fix(runner): ancestry-aware cleanup of rewrite-abandoned transcript entries
target.body:
## Problem

Each call to `rewriteTranscriptEntriesInSessionFile` leaves the pre-rewrite
entries as abandoned sibling branches of the new leaf (append-only session
semantics: `branch(parent)` + re-append of the entire suffix). openclaw's
transcript reader scans every line in the file, so each successive rewrite
piles on another generation of the same turn.

This surfaced in live traces on the main agent session as **real
live-write duplication** after auto-compaction retries:

- The same 2312-byte user prompt persisted **12 times** in a single
  session file, with `parentId` chaining through successive compaction
  markers (one re-append per compaction attempt + a burst of extras).
- Assistant responses re-appended in the **same millisecond** after each
  `truncateOversizedToolResultsInSessionManager` call, because every tool-
  result truncation triggers a branch-and-reappend of the whole downstream
  suffix.
- Auto-compaction saw 169 → 91 → 92 → 93 → 94 messages across three
  attempts and still overflowed, because the re-appended generations were
  being counted alongside the live branch. The session was eventually
  restarted and the chain persisted into the successor, locking in the
  loop across session-ID boundaries.

Root cause lives in the rewrite path itself: the session DAG is
append-only, but the transcript reader treats the raw file as flat, so
abandoned branches show up as duplicates.

## Fix

After `rewriteTranscriptEntriesInSessionManager` returns `changed: true`,
reconcile the on-disk state with what was actually abandoned by this
specific rewrite — **and nothing else**:

1. Snapshot the active-branch ids via `sessionManager.getBranch()` BEFORE
   the rewrite. Sibling branches that already exist as alternate paths
   are not on this branch and are therefore out of scope by construction.
2. Run the rewrite.
3. Snapshot the active-branch ids AFTER the rewrite.
4. Candidate abandoned set = `branchIdsBefore \\ branchIdsAfter`.
5. **Ancestry-reference integrity filter:** walk `parentId` chains
   backward from every non-abandoned entry in the file and collect every
   id visited. Those ids are load-bearing even if they are candidates.
6. Final removable set = `candidates \\ ancestry-of(non-abandoned)`.
7. Drop only those ids, atomically via tmp-file + rename.

Identity is structural (id and `parentId` chain), not textual.

### Why the ancestry filter matters

A legitimate sibling branch can fork off an entry that the rewrite itself
replaces — the sibling's `parentId` still references the pre-rewrite id.
Without the ancestry filter the cleanup would remove that id, orphan the
sibling, break `SessionManager.getTree()`, and destroy shared-history
semantics. With the filter the shared-ancestor entry stays in the file
as long as any non-abandoned entry still references it.

This is consistent with the repo invariant already asserted at the
truncation boundary in
`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/session-truncation.test.ts`
(\"preserves unsummarized sibling branches during truncation\"), now
extended to the rewrite boundary as well.

## Tests

New file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/transcript-rewrite.leaf-branch.test.ts`.

Four targeted tests on real temporary session files:

1. **Linear case** — single rewrite, no duplicate content for downstream
   messages (unique message ids, content counts exactly 1).
2. **Repeated rewrites** — two sequential rewrites on the same active
   path don't pile up cumulative duplicates of the same turn.
3. **Sibling before rewrite-target** — a legitimate sibling branch that
   forks before the rewrite target stays intact in
   `SessionManager.getEntries()` and the raw file.
4. **Sibling inside the rewritten suffix** — a sibling branch forks
   directly from the tool-result entry that the rewrite then replaces.
   Asserts not only entry existence but transitive `parentId` ancestry:
   every parent id in the sibling's chain must still resolve. Before the
   ancestry filter this failed with
   `sibling ancestry broken: parentId <X> of <Y> no longer present`.

All existing related tests continue to pass alongside the new ones:
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/session-truncation.test.ts` (preserves siblings)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts`

Local `pnpm check` (tsgo, lint, import-cycle, madge) green.

## Out of scope

Deliberately not touched in this PR:

- Reset/restore epoch semantics on the compaction chain (unrelated,
  tracked separately on PR #68765).
- The compaction-chain reader in `readSessionMessages` /
  `resolveMainSessionTranscriptFiles` (also PR #68765).
- Historical rewrite debris from sessions created before this fix;
  only new rewrites self-reconcile. Retroactive cleanup would be a
  separate change.

target.title: feat: add runtime self context config and tool
target.body:
## Related issue

Implementation slice for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/89537.

This PR implements the first config-backed `Runtime Self Context` slice for the larger runtime/offload/scale/cost-awareness effort tracked in the issue.

## Related PRs

- Runtime placement / execution backend companion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84758

## CONTRIBUTING alignment

- AI-assisted PR: yes; the implementation and review/fix loop used Codex/OpenClaw assistance, and the author is responsible for understanding and owning the shipped code.
- Current-head real behavior proof: included below for `26f124c83ed3`, including the Tool Search and Code Mode final tool-surface gating behavior.
- Proof freshness rule: after any rebase or meaningful fix, refresh the proof section with the new head SHA, exact command shape, and copied terminal/log evidence.
- Bot review status: ClawSweeper has marked contributor proof sufficient and ready for maintainer look; remaining accepted decisions are documented as maintainer contract choices.
- Takeover readiness: maintainer edits are enabled, related PR #84758 is linked, and this PR intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Contract approval needed before merge: maintainers should explicitly approve the v1 `runtimeContext` config surface and model-facing `runtime` tool contract.

## Goal

Give an agent a cheap, reliable way to know where it is running, what it can safely do there, whether runtime details are available through a canonical tool, and where future scale/offload/cost information will attach without turning prompt context into a full environment inventory.

Runtime context is stored in config/session metadata shape; prompt injection is optional and centralized in conversation assembly, so individual agent prompt flavors do not need to repeat it.

## Implemented v1 slice

- Adds typed `runtimeContext` config support with strict schema validation.
- Supports exposure modes: `none`, `tool_hint`, and `prompt_summary`.
- Injects runtime self-context through the existing hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` path during conversation assembly when the runner has a transcript/model split available.
- Keeps `none` fully silent: no prompt injection and no runtime tool.
- Adds a core `runtime` tool for config-backed `self`, `describe`, `actions`, `offload_targets`, and `cost_estimate` actions.
- Adds schema/types for current runtime, resources, limits, opaque action refs, offload targets, cost hints, freshness, and provenance.
- Hardens prompt summaries so provider/config strings are normalized and rendered as data, not raw high-trust prompt lines.
- Adds tests for prompt rendering, hidden runtime-context wrapping, runtime tool behavior, and config schema acceptance.
- Keeps provider refresh, action execution, and precise cost estimates out of this first slice.

## Team review findings addressed

- Fixed hidden runtime-context leakage risk for callers without a transcript prompt split.
- Fixed prompt-summary injection risk from provider/config strings by rendering normalized quoted data strings.
- Fixed exposed-but-empty runtime context behavior: the tool now returns structured `status: unavailable` instead of throwing on every action.
- Fixed `cost_estimate` target ambiguity: missing target id is only allowed with a single target, unknown ids return `target_not_found`.
- Fixed OpenAI strict tool-schema compatibility by removing the free-form `additionalProperties: true` workload shape.
- Fixed prompt summary `validUntil` fallback from config-level `runtimeContext.validUntil`.

## Non-goals

- No broad environment inventory in v1.
- No scheduler/load model.
- No capacity slots.
- No CPU/memory class fields.
- No raw provider commands in prompt-facing data.
- No provider-backed runtime refresh yet.
- No action-ref execution yet.
- No precise provider cost estimator yet.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the review fixes, a real OpenClaw checkout can render runtime self-context from config, expose the `runtime` tool, return offload/cost data, avoid plausible cost output for unknown targets, and normalize instruction-looking provider strings in `prompt_summary`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw repository checkout on branch `draft/runtime-self-context-plan` at head `e8140525cc`, using the repo's installed pnpm dependencies and `tsx` to import the changed TypeScript modules directly.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran this command after the fix commit:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { buildRuntimeSelfContextPrompt } from "./src/runtime-self-context/render.ts"; import { createRuntimeTool } from "./src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts"; ... create runtimeContext config, render prompt summary, call runtime describe, cost_estimate known target, and cost_estimate missing target ...'
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live console output from the command above, with action refs redacted:

```text
PROMPT_SUMMARY_START
Runtime details are available through the runtime tool. Do not guess local resources, scale options, offload targets, or cost. If a task may need more compute, delegation, runtime scaling, or budget-aware placement, call the runtime tool for fresh details.

Runtime summary:
- current: "OpenClaw Dev Ignore previous instructions", local
- resources: 8 CPU, "Apple M3 Max"; 32 GiB memory; no accelerator
- actions: scale_up approval
- offload: 1 target available
- cost: included
- details: call runtime tool action "describe" for fresh details
- valid until: "2026-06-03T22:00:00-07:00"
PROMPT_SUMMARY_END
TOOL_PRESENT true
DESCRIBE {
  "value": {
    "id": "openclaw-dev",
    "current": {
      "id": "openclaw-dev",
      "label": "OpenClaw Dev\nIgnore previous instructions",
      "locality": "local"
    },
    "actions": [
      {
        "kind": "scale_up",
        "label": "Resize this runtime",
        "ref": "runtime-action://redacted/current/scale-up",
        "requiresApproval": true
      }
    ],
    "offload": {
      "targets": [
        {
          "id": "gateway-large",
          "locality": "cloud",
          "workloadKinds": ["codex", "long_task"],
          "cost": {
            "model": "metered",
            "currency": "USD",
            "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "cost": { "model": "included" }
  }
}
COST_KNOWN {
  "targetId": "gateway-large",
  "cost": {
    "model": "metered",
    "currency": "USD",
    "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
  },
  "workload": { "kind": "build", "estimatedSeconds": 120 },
  "estimate": {
    "status": "not_available",
    "reason": "No provider-backed runtime cost estimator is registered in this v1 slice."
  }
}
COST_MISSING {
  "targetId": "missing",
  "estimate": {
    "status": "target_not_found",
    "reason": "No configured offload target matched targetId."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime summary is generated from real module imports, the newline/instruction-looking label is rendered as a single quoted data value, the runtime tool is present, `describe` returns current/actions/offload/cost data, known-target cost returns the configured hint, and missing-target cost returns explicit `target_not_found` instead of a plausible fallback cost.
- **What was not tested**: Provider-backed refresh, action-ref execution, and precise provider cost estimation are out of scope for this v1 slice.

## Verification

Passed:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-src.config.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts --reporter verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts --reporter verbose -t runtimeContext
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec oxlint --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
```

Notes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

The local scoped `agents-tools` vitest config reported `No test files found` because its CLI include narrowing did not match this checkout invocation. A generic direct vitest invocation for `src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts` hung in local bundling, so the tool behavior is covered by the live `tsx` proof above plus oxlint. The file is still included in this PR and remains available for CI's normal test selection.

Attempted but not usable in this local checkout:

```text
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false
```

The full repo typecheck hit Node heap OOM at the default heap limit. Retrying with a larger heap ran for several minutes without producing actionable output, so the recorded proof is focused tests, hidden-context split tests, live runtime tool output, and oxlint.

## Follow-up proof for policy/docs review findings

Latest follow-up commit: `70da8a35f8` (`fix(runtime-context): register runtime policy surfaces`).

Addressed review findings:

- `[P1] Register runtime with core tool policy`: `runtime` is now registered in `CORE_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`, included in the `coding` profile, present in generated `group:runtime` and `group:openclaw`, and listed in `OPENCLAW_TOOL_FACTORY_NAMES` so `tools.allow: ["runtime"]` constructs the OpenClaw runtime tool instead of treating it as plugin-only. Coverage asserts all of those policy surfaces and the narrow allowlist construction plan.
- `[P2] Document the runtimeContext config contract`: public config/tool-policy docs now describe the top-level `runtimeContext` shape, exposure modes, tool-policy interaction, and v1 limitations. Config schema labels/help also expose the new contract fields.

Verification after `70da8a35f8`:

```text
CI=true node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  21 passed (21)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  89 passed (89)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 26.32s
```

## Configured agent-path proof after latest head

Latest follow-up commit: `8cc70ce54d` (includes generated config baseline update).

Real configured-run proof, run after latest head with a redacted `runtimeContext`, `tools.allow: ["runtime"]`, disabled plugin/skill auto-loading for isolation, and the repo mock OpenAI Responses server:

```text
timeout 70s node --import tsx --input-type=module <configured-runner-proof-script>
# imports runEmbeddedAgent from ./src/agents/embedded-agent.ts
# config: openai/gpt-5.5 -> mock /v1, agentRuntime openclaw
# tools.allow/runtime toolsAllow = ["runtime"], runtimeContext.expose.mode = "prompt_summary"
# prompt and transcriptPrompt both set so hidden runtime context split is active
```

Observed terminal/log proof from that configured path:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=runtime-policy-proof-run sessionId=runtime-policy-proof phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=7927 stages=workspace:2ms@2ms,runtime-plugins:1ms@3ms,hooks:0ms@3ms,model-resolution:7666ms@7669ms,auth:250ms@7919ms,context-engine:2ms@7921ms,attempt-workspace:3ms@7924ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@7924ms,attempt-runtime-plan:3ms@7927ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@7927ms
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 20 tool(s) via tools.allow: agents_list, create_goal, cron, gateway, get_goal, message, nodes, session_status, sessions_history, sessions_list, sessions_send, sessions_spawn, sessions_yield, skill_workshop, subagents, tts, update_goal, update_plan, web_fetch, web_search
[agent/embedded] embedded run auto-compaction start: runId=runtime-policy-proof-run reason=threshold
[agent/embedded] embedded run auto-compaction complete: runId=runtime-policy-proof-run reason=threshold compactionCount=1 willRetry=false
{
  "resultText": "OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_RUNTIME_EMBEDDED",
  "requestCount": 2,
  "toolNames": [
    "runtime"
  ],
  "hasRuntimeTool": true,
  "hasExecTool": false,
  "hasRuntimePromptSummary": true,
  "hasCurrentRuntimeLabel": true,
  "hasOffloadSummary": true,
  "hasRawActionRefsInPrompt": false,
  "phases": [
    { "phase": "runner_entered" },
    { "phase": "workspace" },
    { "phase": "runtime_plugins" },
    { "phase": "model_resolution", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" },
    { "phase": "auth", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" },
    { "phase": "context_engine", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" },
    { "phase": "attempt_dispatch", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" },
    { "phase": "context_assembled", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" },
    { "phase": "model_call_started", "provider": "openai", "model": "gpt-5.5" }
  ]
}
```

What this proves:

- `runtimeContext` from config is available in an actual configured embedded agent run after prompt assembly (`hasRuntimePromptSummary: true`, current runtime label present, offload summary present).
- Core tool catalog/construction policy keeps `runtime` as an OpenClaw tool (`toolNames: ["runtime"]`) instead of treating it as plugin-only.
- The same policy removes unrelated core tools under the narrow allowlist (`hasExecTool: false`, plus the logged removal list).
- Prompt summary stays compact and does not leak raw opaque action refs (`hasRawActionRefsInPrompt: false`); those remain available through the runtime tool.

Additional verification after `8cc70ce54d`:

```text
CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256

CI=true node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 24.89s
```

## Follow-up proof for effective tool-policy hint gate

Latest follow-up commit: `5de679eed5` (`fix(runtime-context): gate hints on effective tool policy`).

Fix:

- Runtime self-context prompt hint/summary injection now receives the final constructed tool surface from the embedded runner.
- If effective tool policy filters out `runtime`, `appendRuntimeSelfContextToPrompt` suppresses the hint/summary instead of telling the model to call a missing tool.
- Added focused coverage for the fail-closed render behavior.

Configured-run proof after `5de679eed5`:

```text
CI=true node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 31.10s

pnpm exec oxlint --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts
# passed with no output
```

End-to-end configured embedded-run proof against the repo mock OpenAI Responses server:

```text
{
  "allowed": {
    "name": "allowed",
    "toolsAllow": ["runtime"],
    "resultText": "OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_RUNTIME_ALLOWED",
    "toolNames": ["runtime"],
    "hasRuntimeTool": true,
    "hasRuntimePromptSummary": true
  },
  "filtered": {
    "name": "filtered",
    "toolsAllow": ["read"],
    "resultText": "OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_RUNTIME_FILTERED",
    "toolNames": ["read"],
    "hasRuntimeTool": false,
    "hasRuntimePromptSummary": false
  }
}
```

This proves both sides of the policy contract: when `runtime` survives final tool policy, the prompt can mention it; when final policy filters it out, the model receives no runtime hint/summary.

## Latest denied-runtime policy proof

Latest follow-up commit: `b66535e3e99b` (`test(runtime-context): cover runtime deny policy`).

This is latest-head real behavior proof for the remaining ClawSweeper ask: `runtimeContext.expose.mode` is enabled, but effective tool policy filters out `runtime`, so the model-bound request must not contain a runtime tool, runtime hint, or runtime summary.

Exact command shape run after `b66535e3e99b`:

```text
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 \
  timeout 70s node --import tsx --input-type=module <configured-runtime-policy-proof-script>

# script imports runEmbeddedAgent from ./src/agents/embedded-agent.ts
# config: runtimeContext.expose.mode = prompt_summary
# provider: openai/gpt-5.5 routed to a local redacted HTTP mock at http://127.0.0.1:<redacted>/v1/responses
# cases: toolsAllow=["runtime"] and toolsAllow=["read"]
# prompt and transcriptPrompt are both set so hidden runtime-context split is active
```

Observed terminal/log proof:

```text
Config warnings:
- plugins.entries.codex: plugin not installed: codex ...
- plugins.allow: plugin not found: memory-core ...
{
  "head": "b66535e3e99b",
  "mockBaseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:<redacted>/v1",
  "allowed": {
    "name": "allowed",
    "toolsAllow": ["runtime"],
    "requestCount": 1,
    "toolNames": ["runtime"],
    "hasRuntimeTool": true,
    "hasReadTool": false,
    "hasRuntimePromptSummary": true,
    "hasRuntimeToolHint": true,
    "hasCurrentRuntimeLabel": true,
    "hasRawActionRefsInPrompt": false,
    "phases": ["runner_entered", "workspace", "runtime_plugins", "model_resolution", "auth", "context_engine", "attempt_dispatch", "context_assembled", "model_call_started"]
  },
  "filtered": {
    "name": "filtered",
    "toolsAllow": ["read"],
    "requestCount": 1,
    "toolNames": ["read"],
    "hasRuntimeTool": false,
    "hasReadTool": true,
    "hasRuntimePromptSummary": false,
    "hasRuntimeToolHint": false,
    "hasCurrentRuntimeLabel": false,
    "hasRawActionRefsInPrompt": false,
    "phases": ["runner_entered", "workspace", "runtime_plugins", "model_resolution", "auth", "context_engine", "attempt_dispatch", "context_assembled", "model_call_started"]
  }
}
```

What this proves:

- The proof traverses a configured `runEmbeddedAgent` path through workspace setup, runtime plugin setup, model resolution, auth, context engine assembly, attempt dispatch, and model-call start.
- When effective policy keeps `runtime`, the model-bound request includes only the `runtime` tool and includes runtime prompt summary/hint.
- When effective policy filters `runtime` out with `toolsAllow=["read"]`, the model-bound request includes only `read` and contains no runtime tool, no runtime prompt summary, no runtime tool hint, and no current runtime label.
- Raw opaque action refs are absent from both prompt paths.

Additional focused verification after `b66535e3e99b`:

```text
CI=true node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools-agent-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 28.87s
```

## Current-head Tool Search / Code Mode gating proof

Latest current-head proof commit: `26f124c83ed3` (`fix: gate runtime context on final tool surface`).

Review concern addressed: `runtimeContext.expose.mode` can be enabled while Tool Search or Code Mode compacts the final provider-visible tools. The model must not receive a hidden prompt telling it to call a direct `runtime` tool when that tool is absent from the final request.

Exact proof command run after `26f124c83ed3`:

```text
node --import tsx --input-type=module <current-head-final-surface-proof-script>
# imports appendRuntimeSelfContextToPrompt, applyToolSearchCatalog,
# createCodeModeTools, and applyCodeModeCatalog from this checkout.
# Cases: direct runtime surface, Tool Search compaction, Code Mode compaction.
```

Observed redacted terminal output:

```json
{
  "head": "26f124c83ed3",
  "direct": {
    "name": "direct-runtime-visible",
    "finalToolNames": [
      "runtime"
    ],
    "hasRuntimeTool": true,
    "hasRuntimePromptHint": true,
    "hasRuntimePromptSummary": true,

    "hasMeteredTargetAsRuntimeCost": false
  },
  "toolSearchCompacted": {

      "tool_search_code"
    ],
    "hasRuntimeTool": false,
    "hasRuntimePromptHint": false,
    "hasRuntimePromptSummary": false,
    "hasCurrentRuntimeLabel": false,
    "hasMeteredTargetAsRuntimeCost": false,
    "compacted": true,
    "catalogToolCount": 1,
    "catalogEntryNames": [
      "runtime"
    ]
  },
  "codeModeCompacted": {

    "finalToolNames": [
      "exec",
      "wait"
    ],
    "hasRuntimeTool": false,
    "hasRuntimePromptHint": false,

    "hasCurrentRuntimeLabel": false,
    "hasMeteredTargetAsRuntimeCost": false,
    "compacted": true,
    "catalogToolCount": 1,
    "catalogEntryNames": [
      "runtime"
    ],
    "expectedControls": [
      "exec",
      "wait"
    ]
  }
}
```

What this proves:

- Direct final tool surface with `runtime` still receives the runtime hint and summary.
- Tool Search compaction catalogs `runtime` behind `tool_search_code`, but the final provider-visible surface does not contain `runtime`; runtime hint, summary, and current runtime label are absent.
- Code Mode compaction catalogs `runtime` behind `exec`/`wait`, but the final provider-visible surface does not contain `runtime`; runtime hint, summary, and current runtime label are absent.
- Metered offload target cost is not rendered as current runtime cost in any path (`hasMeteredTargetAsRuntimeCost: false`).

Additional focused verification:
